quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste-based violence moved over the board, bringing with it a torrent of claims and counter-claims.

Anon discussed specific incidents, including a 1979 attack in Parasbigha and a 1986 event in Chhotki Chhechani village, with some posters citing numbers of dead and alleged perpetrators.

A high-pressure ridge of caste hatred developed, leading to a flurry of ranting and inflammatory comments. One thread, started by an anon seeking blessings, quickly degenerated into caste-based invective.

Meanwhile, a scattered drizzle of pop culture discussion persisted, with one anon suggesting a Bollywood movie featuring Hindu goddesses in bikinis and another discussing the decomposition of an eldritch horror image.

articles.title: Anon Claims Caste-Based Violence, Brags of Atrocities in Bihar
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread saying 'roid maxx body banake rethard chalate hue larp karna aasan hai', which roughly translates to 'it's easy to pretend to be tough after taking steroids'.

One anon responded by bringing up the Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) and their alleged ties with certain militia groups. The RJD had inducted Baiju Yadav, a commander of the Yadav militia Lorik Sena, into the party and the government in the 1990s.

Another anon mentioned that RJD had banned the Ranvir Sena, an upper-caste militia, in 1995. Anon started exchanging claims and counter-claims about caste-based violence in Bihar. One claimed that Yadav Naxals and Lorik Sena killed more Chamars and Bhumihars than others could.

Another anon bragged about their family's role in killing Chamars during the peak caste wars in the 1970s and 1990s, stating 'my family members literally killed chamars like you during peak caste wars in 70s and again in 90s.' Anon discussed various incidents, including the Bara massacre in 1992, where the Maoist Communist Centre of India killed 40 Bhumihars.

They also mentioned the Ranvir Sena and its actions.

Anon joked about the relative strength of different groups, saying 'Bihar Gangsters were using night vision and LMGs, not even lundian military had during that time.' The conversation also touched on the perceived strength and actions of various castes and groups in Bihar, with anon stating 'Rajpust bhumihar brahmin yadav chamar, everyone is loser who kept fighting among themselves'.

Another simply concluded 'Kek and winner of all this shitshow is Koeri kushwaha kurmi'. The discussion remained focused on these claims and counter-claims, with anon boasting about their own actions and those of their families.

Anon reacted to the discussion, saying 'Holy fucking shit, Bihar CHADS I KNEEL'. The thread remained centered on these exchanges, with no clear resolution or consensus.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Blessings, Thread Erupts in Caste-Based Ranting
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a brief message, "Seek blessings anons". This prompt was met with a torrent of replies, many of which devolved into caste-based ranting and insults.

One anon referred to another as "poonga" and claimed their ancestors had "cucked India".

Another anon argued that certain castes, such as Jaats, have a right to worship Indra, and that all castes with "high steppe and high zagros" should do so.

This anon also called for the slaughter of AASI adherents and their "black gods" by the "wraith of Indra". The thread quickly descended into further vitriol, with multiple anons trading insults and accusations.

One anon referred to Sikhi as "fake" and its gurus as "faggots", while another called Baba Saheb a "lundgrabber".

The discussion showed no signs of abating, with anons continuing to exchange hostile messages and personal attacks.

articles.title: Anon discusses decomposition of eldritch horror image
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a two-line post: 'Right side pic context?

Eldritch horror.' One anon replied with an image reference and a claim about a Malayali woman's appearance, saying she looked like 'avg tamil woman.' Another anon shared a personal anecdote about a Malayali girl who was in an arranged marriage but had an affair with a roommate.

The conversation turned to a discussion of physical appearance and intercultural relationships. Anon noted that if Malayali girls 'did not look like shit,' they might be more acceptable. Anon brought up a point about the girl's height, stating she was 5'4".

Another anon expressed amusement at the situation, joking about 'abbos' not controlling their women. The thread also included some off-topic remarks about unrelated subjects.

articles.title: Caste violence incidents mentioned
articles.body:
Anon listed several incidents of caste violence: in 1979, 11 people including one Yadav were killed by Bhumihars in Parasbigha; in 1986, seven Yadavs were killed by Rajput raiders in Chhotki Chhechani village; and 19 Dalits were killed by Bhumihar landlords.

Anon responded with a comment about OBC and SC "forgetting their place". Another anon described a scenario of OBC "bootlicking" Dalits and upper castes raping them.

Replies also included a comment characterising Chamars as "biomass used by governments to farm votes", and a seemingly unrelated exclamation of "JAY HO BABHAN DEVTA".

articles.title: Anon Suggests Bollywood Movie Featuring Hindu Goddesses in Bikinis
articles.body:
Anon posted about a hypothetical movie, 'yrf spy universe,' with main characters Sita and Durga in bikinis, and producer Aditya Chopra, a Khatri from Pakistan Punjab.

Another anon responded with a 'black pill' about Bollywood's history, claiming it was established by families who ran kothas in Lahore.

Anon also joked about wanting more sexo scenes in movies, saying it should be normalized like bikinis. Some anons exchanged insults, with one calling another 'retard' and 'pajeet.'
